subject: First Aid & Safety
title: What Is Safety Culture?
description: A short lesson to help you apply What Is Safety Culture?.

hook.text: At DuPont, the CEO's office has no reserved parking—everyone walks the same safe path inside.

buildup.text: Safety culture is how an organization thinks about and prioritizes safety at every level.

discovery.text: In strong safety cultures, workers stop unsafe acts without fear of punishment or mockery.

twist.text: Company policies mean nothing if supervisors pressure workers to skip safety steps for speed.

climax.text: Culture is built by what leaders do—not what they say—when no one's watching.

punchline.text: Safety culture isn't a poster on the wall. It's what happens on the floor.

quiz.question: What primarily shapes an organization's safety culture?
options: What leaders consistently do, not what they say
